properties.name: Vintage Meadows
properties.description:
We're a small, farm-to-table, family farm located in Goshen, Indiana. We feed families all the way from Elkhart County, IN to Northwest Indiana, Chicago, and the suburbs. Here on our farm we raise 100% grass-fed beef cattle, and pastured, forage & non-gmo fed pork. We co-op with other local family farms to produce the additional products we offer. Each farm we partner with is committed to using our strict protocol of environmentally and organically friendly farming practices.
We are members of the Weston A. Price Foundation, and our goal is to follow their principles of producing nutrient-dense and satisfying food.
Our commitment to you: ✓no antibiotics ✓no vaccines ✓no added hormones ✓non-GMO.
Here at Vintage Meadows, we have a vision to make meaningful connections with people on our farm. We want our farm to become a place where you can slow down, connect with the land, see the animals, learn how we raise them, and get to know the people behind your food. For more info and how to shop our online store, please check out our website at www.vintagemeadows.farm. Thanks!

properties.name: Flowers Creamery
properties.description:
Our world class 6000 sqft small batch artisan cheese and non homogenized traditional whole milk production facility sits on our small family owned dairy south of Nashville in Middle Tennessee. We are certified humane, win awards for our sustainability practices, and offer all natural gourmet products while utilizing cutting edge technologies and procedures.

We, Brian and Morgan Flowers, are both native Tennesseans. Brian grew up learning to farm with his dad here in Lynnville. I grew up close to Nashville and went to law school before deciding I'd rather be on the farm. Brian bootstrapped Flowers Dairy Farms from the ground up in the 1990s and the Creamery has gone from startup to being a vendor at NFL games and wholesaling to over 30 retailers in one and a half years.

Giles County is known as "the land of milk and honey" but there are only two surviving dairies, down from hundreds a generation ago. Despite the dissapearance of the American dairyman, October was our farm’s 25th anniversary! We farm 1,000 acres and milk 150 cows—Holstein and Jersey. That may sound like a lot, but compared to most remaining dairies, we are very small. This means we are able to personally care for each cow every day.

We offer farm tours to show people how and why we farm the way that we do, and to educate people about agriculture and where their food comes from. We also love to be transparent and accountable to our community and our customers. People travel to our farm to visit from all over Tennessee and Alabama--we've even had folks in from Vermont, Wisconsin, Florida, Texas, etc.

We are a USDA permitted facility, so all of our products are top quality human grade food. We only use Tennessee Milk from our on site Grade A Dairy.

Please check out our website, flowerscreamery.com or visit us for more information.

properties.name: 5Heart Earthworm Farm
properties.description:
At 5Heart Earthworm Farm, we’re passionate about reducing waste. The farm is set up on a family farm in Birch Run, Michigan, where food scraps from our residential and business customers are turned into nutrient-rich worm castings. These castings are packaged on the farm and ready to sell shortly after harvesting them!
Sarah Archer, owner of 5Heart Earthworm Farm, has been having worms eat her food scraps for over 20 years and loves to teach about the benefits of using worm castings in your garden, flower beds, or in your potted plants. In 2013, she went from being a worm composting practitioner to a worm farmer. From there, she and her husband Darrell set out to establish 5Heart Earthworm Farm.

properties.name: Windchime Farm
properties.description:
Welcome to Windchime Farm, a woman- and veteran-owned small acreage polyculture operation committed to providing healthy, nutrient-dense food using regenerative agricultural practices and humane animal treatment.
Our heirloom produce is grown naturally, without the use of chemical pesticides, herbicides, or fungicides, and our poultry and livestock are never subjected to growth hormones or antibiotics. We use holistic livestock management with minimal human intervention, allowing our ducks, chickens, geese, turkeys and heritage American Guinea Hogs to live their lives as naturally and safely as possible.

properties.name: Hickory Heal Farm
properties.description:
At Hickory Heal Farm, we love people, plants, animals, and the desire to live a holistic lifestyle. With regenerative agriculture at its core, we focus on soil health, improving food production, increasing biodiversity, and climate resilience. We honor God by working respectfully with His creation while growing medicinal herbs, teas, veggies, pasture chickens, pigs, sheep, mini and full-size jerseys, and grass-finished cattle. Part of the farm is Hickory Heal Gardens, a native woodland plant sanctuary of the United Plant Savers Network, serving the purpose of protecting, educating, and reestablishing medicinal plants in Northeast Mississippi. We aim to feed our family, friends, and neighbors mentally, spiritually, and physically with good things.
https://hickoryhealfarm.com/

We sell "forage-fed and forage-finished" red angus beef through our family business. Our retail beef cattle never see a feedlot and receive no grain additives, pesticides on anything they eat, antibiotics, vaccinations, or growth hormones. They forage off our native pastures, crop residues, and cover crop fields.

Our six-generation family farm is attempting to bring health back to our soil naturally through regularly cycling crops and using methods like no-till and utilizing cover crops to maintain better moisture and nutrients in the soil as we rotate crops from season to season.

properties.name: Double D Ranch
properties.description:
Embark on an unforgettable hunting adventure at Double D Ranch, where we promise a seamless experience from the moment you arrive to the time you leave with your prized game. At Double D Ranch, we understand that the quality of the hunt is only part of the equation—the meticulous processing of your game is equally important. That’s why we offer comprehensive game processing services right here on-site.

Our skilled butchers are available to cut and wrap your animal exactly to your specifications, ensuring that your game meat is ready for immediate use. Whether you’re planning to grill, smoke, or freeze your game, we’ve got you covered. You can take your neatly packaged meat straight home and store it in your freezer without any hassle.

Our services start at $100.00 for regular-sized animals. Larger animals are priced accordingly, and will need to hang for proper aging and cooling to ensure the best quality meat.

At Double D Ranch, we pride ourselves on offering a personalized experience. Our hunters can work directly with our on-site butchers to ensure that their game is processed precisely to their specifications. From field to freezer, we’ve got every detail covered.
